action.hypothesis: With the gated K=128 EF=16 architecture now worse across all tested learning-rate alternatives, the next clean stabilization axis is auxiliary support pressure: increasing AUXK_ALPHA from 0.03125 to 0.0625 may improve feature health and residual coverage enough to recover proxy FVU without changing the core architecture or optimization regime.
action.summary: Keep the gated K=128 EF=16 architecture fixed and start a gated AUXK sweep by testing AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625 in proxy.
action.self_review: This follows the exploitation guidance and changes exactly one parameter on the current best full configuration after the LR bracket was effectively exhausted. The main risk is over-regularizing and diluting the main reconstruction objective, but AUXK is the highest-priority remaining single-axis quality probe within gated.
notes_to_memory: The gated EF=16 family now appears fully bracketed on LR, so subsequent exploitation rounds should probe auxiliary regularization and other single-parameter stabilizers rather than keep shrinking or raising the step size.
This round begins a gated AUXK sweep from the best full anchor by increasing AUXK_ALPHA to 0.0625 while holding architecture, LR, and K fixed.
If higher AUXK is also worse, the remaining single-parameter gated exploitation moves are lower AUXK values or a smaller expansion-factor test rather than more architecture churn.
next_hypotheses: If AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625 improves proxy FVU materially, promote to full and compare directly against the current gated K=128 frontier.
If AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625 is worse, test AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 as the next milder regularization probe on the same gated anchor.
If the AUXK sweep fails at both higher and lower values, test EXPANSION_FACTOR=4 to see whether the current gated regime is over-capacitated rather than under-regularized.
